title: 'Fix glasses at Walmart' — Oct 2021 freshman-fall todo stack
html:
TL;DR

  • Multi-strand Apple Note from Oct 19, 2021 (modified Jan 2022) — Yahir is 18, ~5 weeks into OSU freshman year in Corvallis. Title "Fix glasses at Walmart" is again the first-line-as-title ( signature); body cycles through three registers: financial logistics, social repair, identity ambitions, and shopping list.
  • Financial / family: "Do FAFSA with dad" — the explicit moment Yahir + dad sit down for the FY22-23 federal-aid form. "Establish therapy" recurs (carryover from the April Ford prep stack "have dad send a mail to therapist" — six months later, still unestablished).
  • Social: "Yak to Emilio about his trip" — younger brother-call discipline showing up well before the formal "once a week talk with Emilio" in Sept 2022. The pattern was already there in 2021.
  • Identity ambitions: "Butter one biscuit" (idiomatic — focus on a single thing); "Get in on a cool project" — explicit longing for a Grants Pass out freshman to find a community to plug into. Anticipates CCC / OSIG involvement later that year.
  • Shopping: hangars, highlighters, shoes, hooks, notebooks — pure dorm operating-environment continuation of the Oct 3 dorm mod note sixteen days earlier.
  • Same-day companion to the "Setting your heart ablaze" manifesto note — Oct 19 2021 is a double-note day of high-register manifesto + low-register dorm-logistics, the full-spectrum signature.

Source

"Do fafsa with dad / Establish therapy / Yak to Emilio about his trip / Go to one therapy session / Butter one biscuit / Get in on a cool project / Hangars / Highlighters / Shoes / Hooks / Notebooks"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Earliest "establish therapy" anchor in the corpus that Yahir explicitly logs as his own todo (vs. the April note where it was dad's task) — useful for dating the therapy-readiness arc.
  • Confirms FAFSA was filed (or attempted) Oct 2021 with dad — useful for the Ford / financial-aid timeline.
  • Demonstrates the same-day high/low double-note pattern: manifesto + shopping list, fifteen minutes apart.

Open questions

  • Did the FAFSA-with-dad sitdown actually happen? Outcome not corpus-confirmed.
  • "Butter one biscuit" — Yahir-original idiom or borrowed phrase?
